title: 10 Herbal Creams For Fever
intro_desc: Herbal creams for fever can be a natural and soothing way to help your body recover. But did you know that herbal teas can also be a great way to relieve fever? They work by introducing the active ingredients of the herbs into your bloodstream, where they can help to fight off the underlying infection and reduce your body temperature.  For example, Echinacea purpurea is an herb that is known for its immune-boosting properties. It can help to stimulate your body's natural defenses and reduce the severity of fever. Eucalyptus globulus, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe when you have a fever.  Zingiber officinale, or ginger, is another herb that can be used to relieve fever. It has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce pain and discomfort, and it can also help to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea. When you drink a tea made with these herbs, the active ingredients are absorbed into your bloodstream, where they can start to work their magic and help your body recover from the fever.  Drinking herbal teas can bring many benefits to your life, especially when you're feeling unwell. Not only can they help to relieve fever and reduce your body temperature, but they can also help to boost your immune system and reduce the severity of symptoms. Plus, many herbal teas are easy to make at home, so you can have a soothing cup of tea whenever you need it.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and phenolic acids as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties that can help reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea creams have been shown to have a suppressive effect on the production of TNF-alpha, a cytokine that plays a key role in the development of fever. The caffeic acid derivatives in Echinacea purpurea creams have been found to have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with fever. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Echinacea purpurea creams may help alleviate symptoms of fever and promote recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for fever
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler until it reaches 160°F.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the melted coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with the infused coconut oil and stir until the beeswax is melted and the mixture is smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains a compound called eucalyptol, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. Eucalyptol has a cooling effect on the body, helping to reduce fever and relieve aches. The cream also contains other active constituents like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the skin and reduce swelling. When applied topically, eucalyptus globulus creams can help to open up airways and improve circulation, which can help the body to recover from a fever more efficiently. This is because the cream's active compounds can help to break down mucus and promote sweating, two key processes in recovering from a fever.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for fever
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each heating.
Once melted, remove from heat and add the Eucalyptus globulus essential oil and vitamin E oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify completely. This may take about 30 minutes to an hour.
Once solid, your Eucalyptus globulus cream is ready to use. Apply a small amount to the affected area to help relieve fever symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains the active constituents gingerol and shogaol, which are responsible for its fever-reducing properties. Gingerols and shogaols have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and swelling associated with fever. They also have a thermogenic effect, which helps to lower the body temperature. Additionally, the creams contain flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress caused by fever. By combining these properties, Zingiber officinale creams can provide relief from fever symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for fever
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Ginger root, 2 cups of water, and a cheesecloth.
Boil the water and add the dried Ginger root. Let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture using the cheesecloth and squeeze out as much liquid as possible.
Combine 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and the Ginger liquid in a saucepan. Heat until the beeswax melts.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once set, use as a cream for fever relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams contains quinine, a key alkaloid that helps reduce fever. Quinine has a cooling effect on the body, which helps to lower the temperature. The other alkaloids, such as cinchonine and cinchonidine, also contribute to this effect. These compounds have a vasoconstrictive property, which means they narrow the blood vessels, reducing blood flow to the skin and causing a cooling sensation. As a result, Cinchona officinalis creams can be effective in treating fever by bringing down the body temperature.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for fever
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Cinchona officinalis bark, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Grind the dried Cinchona officinalis bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Melt the coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and stir in the ground Cinchona officinalis powder and vitamin E oil until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify completely before use. Store in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including cineole, limonene, and linalool. These compounds have antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which make them effective in reducing fever. Cineole, in particular, helps to thin mucus and relieve congestion, while limonene has decongestant properties that help to clear the airways. Linalool has a soothing effect on the skin, reducing heat and discomfort. By using Melaleuca alternifolia creams, the skin can breathe and sweat more efficiently, helping the body to cool down and recover from fever.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for fever
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil), and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax and carrier oil in the double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Add 2 tablespoons of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the melted mixture and stir well.
Slowly add the distilled water to the mixture, stirring constantly to avoid lumps.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken. Once thickened, pour into containers and store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids and terpenoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce fever by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals. Ginkgo biloba also contains bilobalide, which has a sedative effect that helps calm the body and reduce fever. The antioxidants in ginkgo biloba creams, such as quercetin and isorhapontigenin, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to fever and inflammation. By reducing inflammation and calming the body, ginkgo biloba creams can help alleviate fever and promote a sense of comfort.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for fever
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract in a heat-resistant bowl.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Remove the bowl from the heat and stir in the Ginkgo biloba extract. Let the mixture cool until it reaches 100°F to 110°F (38°C to 43°C).
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and harden completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol and carvacrol as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have antimicrobial and antipyretic properties, which help reduce fever by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause infection. Thymol also has a cooling effect on the body, which can help lower body temperature. Carvacrol, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of fever-related symptoms. By applying Thymus vulgaris creams to the skin, these bioactive compounds can be absorbed, providing relief from fever and its associated discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for fever
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1 tablespoon of dried thyme leaves.
Heat the water in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the beeswax and coconut oil to the water. Stir until the beeswax is melted.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Add the dried thyme leaves and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once set, use as a cream for fever relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosin, camphor, borneol, and borneol acetate as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce fever by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that cause fever. The antiseptic properties also help prevent infections that can worsen fever. The compounds in Rosmarinus officinalis creams have a cooling effect on the skin, which can help bring down body temperature. By reducing inflammation and preventing infections, these creams can provide relief from fever symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for fever
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Rosmarinus officinalis leaves, 2 cups of coconut oil, and a small saucepan.
Heat 1 cup of coconut oil in the saucepan over low heat.
Add 1 cup of Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the coconut oil and let it simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture and let it cool. Then, mix 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of shea butter into the cooled mixture.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Use as a cream for fever relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains linalool and linalyl acetate as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have a cooling effect on the body, which helps to reduce fever. The antispasmodic properties of linalool and linalyl acetate also help to alleviate the muscle cramps and spasms that often accompany fever. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of these compounds can help to reduce swelling and ease the discomfort associated with fever. By applying Lavandula angustifolia creams to the skin, the bioactive constituents can be absorbed and work to help bring down a fever.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for fever
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the melted mixture and let it steep for 2-3 hours. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl.
Add the vitamin E oil to the cooled mixture and stir well. Pour the mixture into a clean container.
Let the mixture cool and solidify. Once solid, your Lavandula angustifolia cream is ready to use. Apply a small amount to the affected area to help soothe a fever.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and saponins. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and relieve discomfort associated with fever. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to have a cooling effect on the body, which can help bring down a high temperature. The flavonoids present in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams also possess antioxidant properties, which can help combat the oxidative stress caused by fever. By reducing inflammation and fighting oxidative stress, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can provide relief from fever symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for fever
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Add shea butter and glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the melted mixture. Stir until well combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F. Stir in vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before use. Apply a small amount to the affected area to help soothe a fever.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce a fever, but it can't prevent it from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe a sore throat and ease body aches. These teas can also help calm the body, which may help bring down a fever.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a soothing way to help with fever, but having them every day may not be a good idea. Some herbs, like peppermint and chamomile, can be safe for regular consumption, but others, like ginkgo biloba and yarrow, might cause problems if used daily. It's best to use them in moderation and for short periods.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with fever relief, but the time it takes for them to show results varies. For some herbs like peppermint and chamomile, you might feel better within 30 minutes to an hour after drinking. For others, like yarrow and elderflower, it might take a few hours or overnight to notice a difference in your temperature.
faq_time: For a fever, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This allows the body to use the tea's benefits throughout the day. Drinking too close to bedtime can make it harder to fall asleep, which is especially important when you're feeling unwell.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for fever. Some herbs can slow down liver function, which can affect how these medications work. This can lead to too much or too little medication in your body. It's best to check the ingredients of your herbal tea to avoid any potential interactions with your medication.
faq_pregnant: When you have a fever while pregnant, it's natural to want to try something to help feel better. Herbal teas can be soothing, but some may not be safe. Peppermint and ginger teas are often okay, but others like chamomile and raspberry leaf may have some risks. It's best to check the ingredients and choose something simple.
faq_breastfeeding: When you have a fever while breastfeeding, herbal teas can be tempting to soothe you. However, some herbs can pass into your breast milk and affect your baby. For example, sage and peppermint can be safe in small amounts, but too much can cause issues. It's best to stick with gentle, well-known herbs like chamomile or lemon balm.
title_2: Fever in elderly: 10 herbal creams to soothe
